content.problemStatement:
Given the coordinates (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) of a point in 2-D plane. Find if it is possible to reach (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) from (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0). The only possible moves from any coordinate (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) are as follows:


Go to the point with coordinates (i+1,j+1)(i + 1, j + 1)(i+1,j+1).
Go to the point with coordinates (i+1,j−1)(i + 1, j - 1)(i+1,j−1)
Go to the point with coordinates (i−1,j+1)(i - 1, j  + 1)(i−1,j+1).
Go to the point with coordinates (i−1,j−1)(i - 1, j - 1)(i−1,j−1).

Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single line of input, two integers x,yx, yx,y.


Output Format
For each test case, print `YES` if it is possible to reach (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) from (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0), otherwise print `NO`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings "yEs", "yes", "Yes" and "YES" will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‑separated integers x and y, the target coordinates.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if the point (x, y) can be reached from (0, 0) using the allowed diagonal moves, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.output:
YES
NO
YES
NO
YES
NO
sampleTestCases.explanation:
A point is reachable iff x and y have the same parity (x+y even).
(0,2): 0+2=2 even → YES.
(1,2): 1+2=3 odd → NO.
(-1,-3): -4 even → YES.
(-1,0): -1 odd → NO.
(-3,1): -2 even → YES.
(2,-1): 1 odd → NO.
(5,5): sum 10 even → YES.\n(4,7): sum 11 odd → NO.\n(-2,-4): sum -6 even → YES.
Both pairs have even sums (0 and 1111111110), so both are reachable.

solution.approach: Each move changes both coordinates by ±1, therefore the parity (even/odd) of x and y flips on every step. Starting from (0,0) (both even), after any number of moves x and y will always have the same parity. Consequently a point (x,y) is reachable iff (x + y) is even (i.e., x and y have the same parity). The algorithm simply checks this condition for each test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.
